# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
"""Shell-based circle packing exploiting corner/edge geometric advantages for n=26"""
import numpy as np

def construct_packing():
    """
    Shell packing: corners + edges + interior.
    4 corners (largest radii - 2 boundary constraints)
    12 edges (medium radii - 1 boundary constraint, 3 per edge)
    10 interior (fill remaining space efficiently)
    """
    n = 26
    c = np.zeros((n, 2))
    
    # 4 CORNERS - optimal distance maximizes corner radius vs edge space
    cd = 0.095
    c[0], c[1], c[2], c[3] = [cd, cd], [1-cd, cd], [cd, 1-cd], [1-cd, 1-cd]
    
    # 12 EDGE CIRCLES - positioned close to edges for larger radii
    ed = 0.055  # distance from edge
    # Bottom/Top edges (3 each)
    c[4], c[5], c[6] = [0.22, ed], [0.50, ed], [0.78, ed]
    c[7], c[8], c[9] = [0.22, 1-ed], [0.50, 1-ed], [0.78, 1-ed]
    # Left/Right edges (3 each)
    c[10], c[11], c[12] = [ed, 0.22], [ed, 0.50], [ed, 0.78]
    c[13], c[14], c[15] = [1-ed, 0.22], [1-ed, 0.50], [1-ed, 0.78]
    
    # 10 INTERIOR - hexagonal-inspired pattern (3-2-2-3 rows)
    c[16], c[17], c[18] = [0.22, 0.22], [0.50, 0.19], [0.78, 0.22]
    c[19], c[20] = [0.35, 0.38], [0.65, 0.38]
    c[21], c[22] = [0.35, 0.62], [0.65, 0.62]
    c[23], c[24], c[25] = [0.22, 0.78], [0.50, 0.81], [0.78, 0.78]
    
    # Compute max radii from border constraints
    r = np.array([min(x, y, 1-x, 1-y) for x, y in c])
    
    # Iterative convergence for proper radius reduction
    for _ in range(30):
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(i+1, n):
                d = np.linalg.norm(c[i] - c[j])
                if r[i] + r[j] > d > 1e-10:
                    sc = d / (r[i] + r[j])
                    r[i], r[j] = r[i]*sc, r[j]*sc
    
    return c, r, np.sum(r)


# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END


# This part remains fixed (not evolved)
def run_packing():
    """Run the circle packing constructor for n=26"""
    centers, radii, sum_radii = construct_packing()
    return centers, radii, sum_radii


def visualize(centers, radii):
    """
    Visualize the circle packing

    Args:
        centers: np.array of shape (n, 2) with (x, y) coordinates
        radii: np.array of shape (n) with radius of each circle
    """
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    from matplotlib.patches import Circle

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))

    # Draw unit square
    ax.set_xlim(0, 1)
    ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
    ax.set_aspect("equal")
    ax.grid(True)

    # Draw circles
    for i, (center, radius) in enumerate(zip(centers, radii)):
        circle = Circle(center, radius, alpha=0.5)
        ax.add_patch(circle)
        ax.text(center[0], center[1], str(i), ha="center", va="center")

    plt.title(f"Circle Packing (n={len(centers)}, sum={sum(radii):.6f})")
    plt.show()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    centers, radii, sum_radii = run_packing()
    print(f"Sum of radii: {sum_radii}")
    # AlphaEvolve improved this to 2.635

    # Uncomment to visualize:
    visualize(centers, radii)
